topic: A Family Member You Are Proud Of

q1.html:
Describe a family member who you are proud of. You should say:
Who this person is
When this happened
What this person did
And explain why you felt proud.

To begin with, I want to talk about my uncle, who is an accomplished (有造诣的 / 成功的) doctor, because I truly look up to (敬仰) him. Actually, he is a very diligent (勤奋的) person who always puts others first. Whenever his patients need help, he is considerate (体贴的) and completely selfless (无私的). In fact, he has a generous (慷慨的) nature and essentially has a heart of gold (心地善良). For example, he will always go the extra mile (加倍努力) to ensure (确保) everyone is safe. I think, seeing his devoted (挚爱的 / 忠诚的) attitude is highly inspiring (鼓舞人心的) to me. To be honest, I felt so proud when he saved a life last year.

q2.html:
Describe a teacher who has helped you learn something important. You should say:
Who this teacher is
What subject they teach
What important thing you learned from them
And explain why this teacher is special to you.

Honestly, I want to talk about my art teacher, Ms. Liu, who is one of the kindest people I have ever met. To begin with, she teaches drawing class at my middle school, and her lessons were always full of energy. Actually, the most important thing I learned from her was to be creative and to trust my own ideas, instead of just copying other people's work. For example, she once asked us to paint our feelings, which was totally new to me. In the end, she truly changed my life by helping me believe in myself.

q3.html:
Describe a neighbor or family friend who is a good person. You should say:
Who this person is
How long you have known them
What kind things they do for others
And explain why you think they are a good person.

To begin with, I would like to talk about Mrs. Chen, who lives right next door to my family. Actually, I have known her for over eight years, ever since we moved into our apartment building. She is a retired lady, but she is always full of energy and a big smile. To be honest, the kindest thing she does is cook warm meals for the elderly neighbors who live alone, especially during the winter. In the end, I think she is a truly good person because her heart is very warm, and she always puts other people before herself.

q1.html:
Q1: What would children do to make their parents proud?

Op Generally speaking, children can make their parents proud by excelling in their education, as academic success is highly valued. Re This is because, parents often sacrifice (牺牲) time to instill (灌输 (价值观)) good study habits in their kids. Ex For instance, when a student achieves top grades, parents truly appreciate (感激 / 欣赏) the immense effort shown.

q2.html:
Q2: What did you do to make your parents proud when you were young?

Op Looking back, I made my parents proud when I decided to follow in someone's footsteps (继承衣钵 / 效法) by learning the piano. Re The primary reason is that, my mother is a musician who wanted me to share her passion. Ex As an example, performing well at my first recital (独奏会) proved that the apple doesn't fall far from the tree (有其父必有其子).

q3.html:
Q3: What advantages of yours make you proud?

Op From my perspective (观点 / 视角), I am most proud of my ability to maintain peace, because it creates harmony (和谐) in my social circles. Re This is largely due to the fact that, resolving disputes (争端) requires immense patience and excellent communication skills. Ex For example, whenever my classmates argue, I step in to offer calm guidance (指导) to resolve the issue.

q4.html:
Q4: When was the last time that you felt proud of yourself?

Op To be specific, I felt incredibly proud last month when I helped bridge a generation gap (代沟) between my grandfather and me. Re This was significant because, younger people and older relatives often struggle to comprehend (理解) each other. Ex As an illustration, I taught him how to use a smartphone, which greatly improved our daily conversations.

q5.html:
Q5: How does family pride contribute to the stability of a society?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, family pride creates a strong foundation, which ultimately leads to a more stable community. Re The core reason is that, people naturally want to protect and support their own flesh and blood (骨肉 / 亲人). Ex For instance, families that respect each other are far less likely to commit crimes (犯罪) in public areas.

q6.html:
Q6: Do you think the definition of "success" within families has changed between the past and the present?

Op Undeniably, the definition of success has evolved (演变), as modern families value personal happiness over just financial wealth. Re In the past, success simply meant being the main breadwinner (养家糊口的人) to provide basic necessities (必需品). Ex Today, however, achieving a healthy work-life balance and pursuing individual passions are seen as equally important.

q1.html:
Describe a foreign country where you'd like to work for a short time. You should say:
Where you would like to go
What kind of work you want to do
When you would like to go
And explain why you want to work in that place.

To be honest, I would love to work in London, which is a cosmopolitan (世界性的 / 国际化的) city, because it offers many excellent opportunities. Personally, I want to immerse (沉浸) myself in the culture and avoid any culture shock (文化冲击). Hopefully, I can find a lucrative (利润丰厚的 / 赚钱的) job in the technology sector soon. I think, working there will genuinely help me broaden one's horizons (开阔眼界). Of course, I am an adaptable (适应性强的) and diligent (勤奋的) person, so I will easily overcome any language barrier (障碍). Even if, I have to start from scratch (白手起家 / 从头开始), it will give me a new perspective (视角) on life.

q2.html:
Describe a country you would like to visit for a holiday. You should say:
Which country it is
What you would like to see or do there
How you first heard about this place
And explain why you want to visit this country.

Honestly, I would really love to visit Thailand for a holiday someday soon. To begin with, it is a beautiful country in Southeast Asia, famous for its tropical beaches and friendly locals. While I am there, I really want to visit the ancient temples in Bangkok and Chiang Mai, because the architecture looks absolutely stunning. Actually, I first heard about Thailand from a travel video on Bilibili last year, and the colourful street markets caught my eye immediately. In the end, I want to visit because I love the food, especially spicy curries, and I think the trip would help me relax completely.

q3.html:
Describe a place you visited where you learned something about a different culture. You should say:
Where this place is
When you went there
What you learned about the culture
And explain how this experience changed your thinking.

To begin with, I want to talk about my visit to the old hutongs in Beijing, which are narrow alleyways full of history. Actually, I went there last winter with my family during the Spring Festival holiday, and the weather was freezing cold. While walking around, I learned a lot about old Chinese traditions, like paper-cutting, tea ceremonies and how neighbours used to share courtyards. Honestly, talking with the elderly residents really opened my eyes to a slower way of life. In the end, this experience made me much more open-minded, and now I truly appreciate traditional culture far more than before.

q1.html:
Q1: What kind of job is easy to get in a foreign country?

Op Generally speaking, jobs in the restaurant sector are the easiest to secure, as they require fewer formal qualifications. Re This is because, these entry-level roles usually welcome a highly diverse workforce. Ex For example, international students often find quick work in local cafes to bypass the initial language barrier (语言障碍).

q2.html:
Q2: Should young people work abroad?

Op In my view, young people should definitely try to work overseas, because it provides an incredibly inspiring (鼓舞人心的) life experience. Re The main reason is that, international travel fosters rapid personal growth and deepens cultural integration (融合 / 一体化). Ex For instance, living in a foreign melting pot (大熔炉 (多种文化融合)) teaches young professionals how to become independent quickly.

q3.html:
Q3: Do Chinese parents encourage their children to work abroad?

Op Admittedly, many Chinese parents do encourage working abroad, as they believe it leads to a more prosperous (繁荣的) future. Re This is due to the fact that, global experience can significantly enhance (提升) a resume in today’s job market. Ex As an illustration, parents often support their children financially so they can benefit from ongoing globalization (全球化).

q4.html:
Q4: Why do some people not want to work overseas?

Op On the other hand, some individuals avoid working abroad, because they fear facing isolation or racial discrimination (歧视). Re Furthermore, leaving behind family and friends can ruin their sense of emotional stability and social harmony (和谐). Ex For example, many people strongly prefer the quiet comfort of their hometown rather than adapting (适应) overseas.

q5.html:
Q5: What are the benefits of working abroad?

Op Without a doubt, working abroad offers immense benefits, as it helps individuals develop vital cross-cultural communication skills. Re This is because, employees must learn local customs, following the idea that when in Rome, do as the Romans do (入乡随俗). Ex Consequently, this cultural flexibility makes them highly valuable to international corporations (大公司) in the long run.

q6.html:
Q6: How does the migration of skilled workers (brain drain) affect the economy of the country they leave?

Op Unfortunately, the phenomenon of brain drain (人才流失) severely impacts a developing nation, because it removes essential talent. Re The primary issue is that, losing highly educated professionals slows down domestic innovation and technological progress. Ex For instance, when top engineers migrate (迁移) to wealthier countries, their home nation struggles to build modern infrastructure (基础设施).

q1.html:
Describe your perfect job. You should say:
What it is
Where you heard about it
What you need to learn to get the job
And explain why you think it is your perfect job.

To begin with, my dream job (理想的工作) is to become an architect, because I am highly adaptable (适应力强的), and I love designing things. Actually, I first heard about this exciting profession from a documentary on television. To get this job, I need to become fully qualified (合格的 / 胜任的) by studying advanced mathematics. I know, it is a very demanding (要求高的 / 费力的) and challenging (充满挑战的) field to enter. However, I want to climb the career ladder (在职业阶梯上晋升) and work for a prestigious (有声望的) company. Ultimately, it is perfect because it is both a lucrative (赚钱的) way to make a living (谋生) and incredibly fulfilling (令人满足的).

q2.html:
Describe a useful skill you would like to learn in the future. You should say:
What the skill is
How you would learn it
Why this skill is useful
And explain how learning this skill would change your life.

Honestly, the skill I would really love to learn in the future is cooking well. To begin with, I think being able to prepare delicious, healthy meals at home is an incredibly valuable life skill. Actually, I would learn it by watching online cooking videos and following step-by-step recipes from famous chefs. In addition, this skill is very useful because it helps me save money and eat much healthier than ordering takeaway. In the end, I believe learning to cook would change my life by making me feel proud, independent, and far more confident every single day.

q3.html:
Describe an important goal you hope to achieve in the next few years. You should say:
What the goal is
Why this goal is important to you
What steps you need to take
And explain how you will feel when you achieve it.

To begin with, an important goal I really hope to achieve in the next few years is to get a university degree. Honestly, this goal matters a lot to me because a good qualification will give me a much better future and more career opportunities. Actually, to make it happen, I need to study every single day, attend all my lectures, and work hard on every assignment. In addition, I plan to ask my teachers for advice whenever I struggle. In the end, I know I will feel very happy, proud, and incredibly grateful when I finally graduate.

q1.html:
Q1: What jobs do children want to do when they grow up?

Op Generally speaking, children often dream of becoming doctors or astronauts, as these roles seem highly heroic (英雄的). Re This is largely because, kids are heavily influenced by the media and the exciting cartoons they watch. Ex For instance, watching a superhero movie might spark the initial motivation (动力 / 动机) to save lives when they are older.

q2.html:
Q2: What should people consider when choosing jobs?

Op In my view, individuals must consider their personal interests, because passion is essential (必要的) for long-term career satisfaction. Re Furthermore, evaluating the level of job security (工作保障) is absolutely crucial in today's unpredictable global economy. Ex For example, choosing a stable industry prevents the constant terrible fear of sudden unemployment (失业) during a financial crisis.

q3.html:
Q3: Is salary the main reason people choose a certain job?

Op Admittedly, salary is a major factor, as everyone needs sufficient income to bring home the bacon (养家糊口). Re However, a high salary alone cannot compensate (补偿) for a toxic work environment or extreme daily stress. Ex Consequently, many employees will actually accept a slightly lower wage if it guarantees a better work-life balance (工作与生活的平衡).

q4.html:
Q4: What kind of jobs are well-paid?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, roles in technology and finance are highly lucrative (赚钱的), because they strictly drive the modern economy. Re The core reason is that, these sectors require specialized knowledge and professionals who can think outside the box (跳出框框思考). Ex For instance, top software developers in prosperous (繁荣的) cities receive massive financial bonuses for creating innovative programs.

q5.html:
Q5: Do people's ideal jobs change as they grow up?

Op Undeniably, career aspirations (抱负) shift drastically over time, as adults gain a much more realistic understanding of the world. Re As people mature, they realize they do not want to be stuck forever in a boring dead-end job (没前途的工作). Ex As an illustration, a teenager might want to be a singer, but later cautiously chooses a stable accounting career instead.

q6.html:
Q6: To what extent does technology (such as AI) threaten traditional job roles in modern society?

Op Without a doubt, the rise of automation (自动化) poses a significant threat, as machines can now perform repetitive tasks perfectly. Re The primary issue is that, this technological shift rapidly accelerates the brain drain (人才流失) in traditional manufacturing sectors. Ex For example, factory workers are frequently replaced by intelligent robots, forcing them to learn completely new digital skills.

q1.html:
Describe a movie you watched recently that you felt disappointed about. You should say:
What it was
Why you decided to watch it
When you watched it
And explain why you felt disappointed about it.

To be honest, I want to talk about a sci-fi movie I saw last weekend, because it was highly anticipated (期待已久的), and I love the actors. Actually, the studio tried to hype up (炒作 / 吹捧) the film with amazing trailers. However, the storyline was completely predictable (可预测的 / 老套的) and deeply confusing (令人困惑的). I think, the acting was very mediocre (平庸的), making the whole experience a huge let-down (令人失望的事). Personally, I felt it was incredibly overrated (被高估的) by the critics. In fact, watching it felt like a complete waste of time (浪费时间) instead of being fulfilling (令人满足的). Ultimately, it failed to meet my demanding (要求高的 / 费力的) expectations for a good film.

q2.html:
Describe a live event or show you attended that was not as good as you expected. You should say:
What the event was
When and where it happened
What you expected before going
And explain why the experience was disappointing.

Honestly, I want to talk about a music concert I attended last summer at an outdoor stadium in my city. To begin with, it was a popular rock band that I had loved for years, so I bought my ticket months in advance. Actually, I expected an unforgettable night full of energy, great songs, and amazing visual effects. However, the sound system was terrible, and you could barely hear the lead singer's voice. In fact, the band also cut their setlist short because of the rain. In the end, I left feeling like the whole experience was a huge let-down.

q3.html:
Describe a product you bought online that you were not happy with. You should say:
What the product was
Where you bought it
Why you chose this product
And explain what was wrong with it.

Honestly, I want to talk about a pair of cheap wireless headphones I purchased a few months ago. To begin with, I bought them from a popular online shop during a big sale, hoping to save some money. Actually, I chose this particular brand because the product page had hundreds of glowing reviews and impressive star ratings. However, when they arrived, the sound quality was surprisingly poor and the bass sounded muddy. In fact, one side completely stopped working after only two weeks of normal use. In the end, I felt the whole purchase was a total waste of time and money.

q1.html:
Q1: Do you believe movie reviews?

Op Generally speaking, I only trust reviews from independent critics, as mainstream websites are often paid for marketing. Re This is because, studios use their massive financial influence (影响) to artificially boost the ratings of a blockbuster (大片 (卖座电影)). Ex For instance, many highly rated action films turn out to be completely boring when you actually watch them.

q2.html:
Q2: What are the different types of films in your country?

Op In my observation, audiences in my country enjoy a wide variety of films, though comedies are particularly beloved. Re The primary reason is that, people seek lighthearted entertainment as a motivation (动机) to relax after working hard. Ex As an example, the romance and martial arts genre (体裁 / 类型) also attract massive crowds during national holidays.

q3.html:
Q3: Are historical films popular in your country? Why?

Op Undeniably, historical films are extremely popular, because they deeply connect the younger generation with their cultural heritage. Re This is largely due to the fact that, these movies often feature stunning traditional cinematography (电影摄影技术) and epic battles. Ex For example, historical stories are frequently a massive box office hit (票房大卖) during the lively Spring Festival season.

q4.html:
Q4: Do you think films with famous actors or actresses are more likely to become successful?

Op Admittedly, having a star-studded cast (众星云集的阵容) guarantees initial ticket sales, as loyal fans will always support their favorite celebrities. Re However, fame alone cannot save a movie if the script is poorly written or the directing is weak. Ex Consequently, relying entirely on famous faces instead of good storytelling is a very risky strategy for modern filmmakers.

q5.html:
Q5: Why are Japanese animated films so popular?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, Japanese animation is globally successful, because it explores deep philosophical themes beautifully. Re Furthermore, their unique visual style easily keeps audiences completely on the edge of one's seat (由于紧张兴奋而坐立不安). Ex For instance, the smart combination of emotional storytelling and modern digital automation (自动化 (used in context of CGI)) creates true visual masterpieces.

q6.html:
Q6: Should directors pay a lot of money to famous actors?

Op In my view, directors should not overpay actors, as this practice severely limits the budget for actual production quality. Re The core issue is that, excessive salaries take away funds needed for proper editing and navigating strict censorship (审查制度) requirements. Ex Therefore, it is much wiser to invest money in talented script writers rather than just paying expensive celebrities.

q1.html:
Describe a person who encouraged you to protect nature. You should say:
Who this person is
How you knew this person
What he/she said or did to encourage you
And explain how you felt about it.

To begin with, I want to talk about my uncle, who is a very conscientious (尽责的 / 有良心的) person, because he constantly teaches me to be eco-friendly (环保的). Actually, he is a truly passionate (热情的) advocate (提倡者) for sustainable (可持续的) living in our hometown. For example, he always tries to do his bit (尽自己的一份力) by picking up litter in the park. I think, he is a genuine nature lover (热爱大自然的人) who makes sure to practice what you preach (言行一致) every day. To be honest, his daily dedication is incredibly inspiring (鼓舞人心的) to me. Therefore, his amazing actions are never predictable (可预测的) and always motivate me.

q2.html:
Describe someone you know who lives a very healthy or natural lifestyle. You should say:
Who this person is
What healthy habits they have
How they started living this way
And explain what you have learned from their lifestyle.

Honestly, I want to talk about my aunt, who lives a remarkably healthy and natural lifestyle. To begin with, she only eats organic food that she buys from the local farmers' market, and she never touches processed snacks. Actually, she also goes jogging every single morning before sunrise. She started living this way around five years ago, after she got really sick and her doctor warned her to change her habits. In the end, what I've learned from her is that small, daily choices truly matter. To be honest, she has inspired me to drink more water and sleep earlier.

q3.html:
Describe a person who does volunteer work to help others or the community. You should say:
Who this person is
What kind of volunteer work they do
How often they do it
And explain why you think their work is important.

To begin with, I'd like to talk about my classmate Lily, who is genuinely one of the kindest people I know. Actually, she spends a lot of her free time cleaning up the local park, picking up litter and helping plant new trees with a small community group. She does this every weekend, no matter the weather, which I find really impressive. In the end, I think her work is incredibly important because it not only keeps our neighbourhood beautiful but also helps the community feel more connected. Honestly, she sets a wonderful example for everyone our age.

q1.html:
Q1: How do people protect the environment?

Op Generally speaking, people protect the environment by recycling daily, because this massively reduces unnecessary waste. Re This is absolutely crucial since, protecting local biodiversity (生物多样性) is necessary for human survival. Ex For instance, even though one person's effort feels like a drop in the ocean (沧海一粟), collective community action makes a huge difference.

q2.html:
Q2: Do you think it's important to protect wild animals/nature?

Op Undeniably, it is vital to protect nature, as wildlife strictly maintains our fragile ecological balance. Re The primary reason is that, failing to prioritize conservation (保护 (自然)) will destroy future food sources. Ex Consequently, we must bridge the generation gap (代沟) to ensure older and younger people work together on this urgent issue.

q3.html:
Q3: What are the main environmental problems in your country?

Op In my view, the most serious problem is severe air pollution (污染), which deeply affects public health. Re This is largely due to the fact that, rapid industrial growth has prioritized profits over clean air. Ex As an illustration, heavy smog in major cities is just the tip of the iceberg (冰山一角) regarding the damage.

q4.html:
Q4: Do you think we should use more renewable energy? Why?

Op Without a doubt, we must aggressively shift to renewable (可再生的) energy, because fossil fuels are rapidly depleting. Re This is absolutely necessary since, traditional dirty energy sources actively wreak havoc (造成严重破坏) on the global climate. Ex For example, utilizing solar panels can significantly reduce our carbon footprint and prevent further deadly temperature increases.

q5.html:
Q5: Should schools teach students about environmental protection?

Op Absolutely, schools must teach environmental protection, as children are the future leaders of our planet. Re The core reason is that, early education effectively fosters a lifelong sense of responsibility towards nature. Ex For instance, teaching students about animal extinction (灭绝) helps them genuinely understand the real-world consequences of human negligence.

q6.html:
Q6: How effective is international cooperation in solving global environmental crises?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, international cooperation is highly effective, because pollution does not respect national borders. Re This essentially means that, a single country's domestic policies cannot solve global warming alone. Ex Therefore, global treaties have a massive influence (影响) on successfully reducing carbon emissions across the entire industrialized world.

q1.html:
Describe a place with a lot of trees that you would like to visit (e.g., a forest, oasis). You should say:
Where it is
How you knew this place
What it is like
And explain why you would like to visit it.

To begin with, I really want to visit a tranquil (宁静的) bamboo forest in Sichuan, because I need to get away from it all (远离尘嚣), which sounds amazing. Actually, I discovered this picturesque (风景如画的) location while reading an eco-friendly (环保的) travel blog online. Apparently, the scenery is completely breathtaking (惊人的 / 极美的) with endless lush (郁郁葱葱的) green trees. I think, it is quite isolated (偏远的 / 孤立的), making it the perfect place to find some peace and quiet (宁静). Furthermore, the local village focuses heavily on sustainable (可持续的) tourism. Ultimately, I want to visit this place because it is totally off the beaten track (人迹罕至的).

q2.html:
Describe a river, lake, or beach that you enjoy visiting. You should say:
Where it is
How often you go there
What you usually do there
And explain why you enjoy spending time at this place.

Honestly, I want to talk about a beautiful lake located just on the outskirts of my hometown. To begin with, it is only a short bike ride away from my apartment, so I try to visit it almost every month. Actually, when I get there, I usually take photos of the scenery, walk slowly around the water, and sometimes sit on a bench reading a book. The atmosphere is very calming, and the fresh air is wonderful. In the end, I really enjoy this place because it helps me relax and forget about all my daily stress.

q3.html:
Describe an outdoor area in your city where people go to relax. You should say:
What the area is
Where it is located
What people do there
And explain why this area is popular for relaxing.

To begin with, I would like to describe a lovely riverside walk that has become really popular in my city. Actually, it is situated right in the city center, running along the main river for a few kilometers. Honestly, you can see all kinds of people there, especially in the evenings. Many residents jog along the path, while others chat with friends, walk their dogs, or simply sit on benches enjoying the view. In the end, I think this area is so popular because it allows people to escape the noise and traffic of the busy downtown streets.

q1.html:
Q1: Why do people like visiting places with trees or forests?

Op Generally speaking, people visit forests to escape stress, as modern life is often overwhelming. Re This is due to the fact that, rapid urbanization (城市化) creates noisy and crowded living conditions. Ex For example, taking a walk among trees is literally a breath of fresh air (令人耳目一新的人或事) for exhausted city workers.

q2.html:
Q2: Are natural views better than city views?

Op In my view, natural views are far superior, because they provide immense psychological benefits. Re The primary reason is that, staring at a concrete jungle (钢筋水泥森林 (城市)) all day increases mental fatigue. Ex As an illustration, looking at green plants has a proven therapeutic (有治疗作用的 / 治愈的) effect on the human brain.

q3.html:
Q3: Do all people need some nature?

Op Absolutely, every human needs access to nature, as we are biologically connected to the earth. Re This is crucial since, humanity evolved in a natural habitat (栖息地) long before modern cities existed. Ex Consequently, getting back to nature (回归自然) on weekends is essential for maintaining our emotional health.

q4.html:
Q4: Are people hard-wired to protect the environment?

Op Admittedly, not everyone is naturally protective, because greed often overrides environmental concerns. Re The core issue is that, massive corporations frequently prioritize profits over the preservation (保护 / 保存) of natural resources. Ex For instance, ongoing deforestation (森林砍伐) for farming proves that many people value money over ecological balance.

q5.html:
Q5: How does rapid urbanization impact the mental health of city dwellers?

Op Undeniably, city expansion heavily impacts mental health, as it separates people from calming green spaces. Re Furthermore, the constant traffic noise and severe pollution (污染) contribute to very high stress levels. Ex Therefore, city residents frequently suffer from anxiety compared to those living in quiet country areas.

q6.html:
Q6: Should governments prioritize economic development over the preservation of natural green spaces?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, governments must balance development and nature, because destroying green spaces is permanent. Re This is necessary since, economic growth means nothing if it destroys local biodiversity (生物多样性). Ex For example, building a factory over a park permanently ruins the community's quality of life.

q1.html:
Describe an application (app) you usually use on your phone or computer. You should say:
What it is
When you use it
Why you use it often
And explain how you feel about it.

To begin with, I want to describe WeChat, which is truly an indispensable (不可或缺的) app, because I use it daily. Actually, this innovative (创新的) platform puts everything right at your fingertips (触手可及). For instance, I use it to chat with friends, ensuring I never feel isolated (孤立的). I think, its clean interface is incredibly user-friendly (用户友好的 / 易用的) and highly efficient (高效的). However, the short videos can be quite addictive (令人上瘾的) and I often lose track of time (忘记时间). Sometimes, I am completely glued to the screen (盯着屏幕看) watching breathtaking (惊人的) travel clips. Overall, I feel that this app has completely transformed my social life.

q2.html:
Describe a website you use often for studying or work. You should say:
What the website is
What you use it for
How often you visit it
And explain why this website is helpful to you.

Honestly, I want to talk about a learning platform called Duolingo, which I rely on almost every single day. To begin with, it is basically a language website where I can practice English through fun, short exercises. Actually, I mainly use it to build my vocabulary and improve my grammar before exams. I visit it every day, usually for around twenty minutes after dinner. In fact, it sends me daily reminders, which keeps me motivated. To be honest, this website is incredibly helpful because the lessons feel like games, so I never lose interest. In the end, it has truly boosted my confidence in speaking English.

q3.html:
Describe a piece of technology that has changed your daily routine. You should say:
What the technology is
When you started using it
How it changed your routine
And explain whether this change has been good or bad.

Honestly, I want to talk about my smart watch, which has completely transformed the way I live. To begin with, it is a small wearable device that tracks my steps, heart rate and sleep quality. Actually, I started using it last birthday, when my parents gave it to me as a surprise gift. Since then, my routine has changed quite a lot, because I now exercise every morning and go to bed earlier. In fact, it reminds me to stand up and stretch whenever I sit too long. In the end, I think this change has been mostly positive, because I feel healthier and more energetic.

q1.html:
Q1: What is the difference between how young and old people use apps?

Op Generally speaking, young people use apps for entertainment, while older generations focus on practical tools. Re This is because, teenagers have a much higher dependency (依赖) on social media for their daily communication. Ex For instance, a young person might act like a social butterfly (交际花) online, whereas grandparents only use banking apps.

q2.html:
Q2: Why do some people dislike using apps?

Op In my view, some individuals dislike apps, because they feel overwhelmed by constant daily notifications. Re The primary reason is that, continuous phone alerts lead to severe information overload (信息过载) and mental fatigue. Ex Furthermore, many older users are deeply concerned that these applications secretly steal their personal privacy (隐私).

q3.html:
Q3: What is the most popular app in your country?

Op Without a doubt, the most popular app is WeChat, as it completely dominates digital communication. Re This is largely due to the fact that, the rapid digitalization (数字化) of society requires an all-in-one platform. Ex For example, it facilitates everything from daily business interaction (互动) to paying utility bills with a single tap.

q4.html:
Q4: Are people becoming too dependent on mobile apps?

Op Admittedly, society has become overly dependent, because apps now control almost every daily task. Re This reliance is a double-edged sword (双刃剑), as it brings great convenience but severely reduces basic survival skills. Ex As an illustration, due to modern urbanization (城市化), many young people cannot navigate their own city without maps.

q5.html:
Q5: To what extent do mobile applications compromise user privacy and data security?

Op Unfortunately, mobile apps heavily compromise data security, as many platforms legally sell user information. Re The core issue is that, greedy companies track our location and browsing habits to generate targeted advertising. Ex Therefore, poor cybersecurity (网络安全) measures often leave individuals completely vulnerable to identity theft and financial fraud.

q6.html:
Q6: Will digital tools eventually replace traditional face-to-face services entirely?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, digital tools will not replace humans, because physical contact remains vital. Re This is necessary since, humans naturally crave empathy and real emotional connection during difficult or stressful situations. Ex For instance, speaking to a real doctor provides a therapeutic (有治疗作用的) comfort that an AI robot simply cannot replicate.

q1.html:
Describe a time when you gave advice to others. You should say:
When it was
To whom you gave the advice
What the advice was
And explain why you gave the advice.

To begin with, I want to talk about giving constructive (建设性的) advice to my friend, because he was very hesitant (犹豫的). Actually, he was reluctant (不情愿的) to study for his IELTS exam. Since we were in the same boat (处境相同), I decided to share a word of wisdom (至理名言) with him. I told him that, creating an efficient (高效的) schedule is truly indispensable (不可或缺的) for success. Luckily, he didn't get angry and really decided to take it to heart (往心里去 (认真对待)). I think, he realized my suggestion was very sensible (明智的). Ultimately, he was extremely grateful (感激的) when he finally passed his test.

q2.html:
Describe a time when someone gave you helpful advice. You should say:
Who gave you the advice
What the advice was about
When they gave it
And explain how this advice helped you.

Honestly, I want to talk about a time when my older sister gave me really helpful advice. To begin with, she sat me down and shared some thoughts about my study habits, because she could see I was struggling. Actually, this happened right before my final exams last semester, when I felt totally stressed out. She suggested I create a daily schedule and review my notes in short sessions instead of cramming. In the end, I took her advice to heart, and I genuinely got much better grades than before. I felt incredibly grateful for her support.

q3.html:
Describe a difficult decision you had to make. You should say:
What the decision was
Why it was difficult
Who helped you decide
And explain how you felt after making the decision.

To be honest, I want to share a difficult decision I made a couple of years ago, which was changing schools. To begin with, my family moved to another city, so I had to transfer to a brand-new high school. Actually, this was really tough because I knew I would miss my close friends terribly and start over alone. Luckily, my parents helped me weigh the pros and cons and reassured me everything would work out fine. In the end, I felt genuinely relieved after making the choice, and I quickly settled in and made wonderful new friends.

q1.html:
Q1: Should people prepare before giving advice?

Op Generally speaking, people should definitely prepare before giving advice, as words can deeply impact others. Re This is because, offering an objective (客观的) perspective requires careful thought and understanding of the situation. Ex For instance, speaking without preparation often leads to careless comments rather than true wisdom (智慧).

q2.html:
Q2: Is it good to ask for advice from strangers online?

Op In my view, asking strangers online is highly risky, because you cannot verify their actual expertise. Re The primary reason is that, trusting anonymous forums is often like the blind leading the blind (问道于盲). Ex Furthermore, sharing detailed personal problems on the internet severely compromises your own digital privacy (隐私).

q3.html:
Q3: What are the personalities of people whose job is to give advice to others?

Op Without a doubt, professionals who give advice possess high emotional intelligence, as their job requires empathy. Re This is crucial since, providing effective counseling (咨询 / 辅导) demands immense patience and active listening skills. Ex As an illustration, a good therapist always maintains a calm and supportive attitude during every client interaction (互动).

q4.html:
Q4: What are the problems if you ask too many people for advice?

Op Admittedly, asking too many people causes total confusion, because everyone will offer a completely different opinion. Re The core issue is that, conflicting advice creates a mental dilemma (进退两难的困境) and paralyzes your decision-making. Ex Consequently, you might end up deciding to just sit on the fence (保持中立 / 观望) forever without acting.

q5.html:
Q5: Is the advice given by older generations still relevant to the problems young people face today?

Op Undeniably, advice from older generations remains highly relevant, as human nature does not change over time. Re Even though technology advances rapidly, the fundamental struggles of human relationships and careers are always the same. Ex Therefore, listening to grandparents prevents young people from having to learn the hard way (通过吃亏学到教训).

q6.html:
Q6: To what extent does professional counseling differ from advice given by friends or family?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, professional advice is strictly evidence-based, whereas friends offer purely emotional support. Re This is necessary since, trained experts use proven psychological strategies for proper intervention (干预) in serious crises. Ex For example, a family member might just agree with you, but a professional will challenge your thoughts.

q1.html:
Describe a child who enjoys drawing/painting. You should say:
How you know this child
How often he/she draws
What he/she likes to draw or paint
And explain why you think he/she likes drawing/painting.

To begin with, I want to talk about my nephew, who is a very imaginative (富于想象力的) child, because he loves painting. Actually, he is incredibly gifted (有天赋的) and clearly possesses a hidden talent (隐藏的天赋) for visual arts. Whenever he holds a brush, he is truly in one's element (如鱼得水) and becomes highly expressive (表现力强的). In fact, he is very observant (观察力敏锐的) and likes to think outside the box (跳出框框思考). Even though, his bedroom gets very messy (凌乱的), his parents are never reluctant (不情愿的) to buy him paint. I think, he enjoys drawing because it allows him to share his feelings. Ultimately, I am grateful (感激的) to see his creativity grow daily.

q2.html:
Describe a young person who is good at playing a musical instrument. You should say:
Who this person is
What instrument they play
How they learned to play
And explain why you think they are talented.

Honestly, I want to talk about my younger cousin, who is only fourteen years old but already a wonderful musician. To begin with, she plays the piano, and she practises almost every single day after school. Actually, she started learning when she was just six, taking weekly lessons from a very patient teacher in our neighbourhood. Over the years, she has won several small competitions in our city. In fact, I think she is truly talented because she can play complex pieces beautifully and even compose her own short melodies. In the end, listening to her perform always gives me goosebumps.

q3.html:
Describe a child you know who is very active and full of energy. You should say:
Who this child is
What activities they enjoy
How they behave around others
And explain why you think this child is special.

Honestly, I want to talk about my neighbour's son, who is around seven years old and absolutely full of energy. To begin with, he loves running around the courtyard, climbing trees, and playing football with the older kids in our building. Actually, he never seems to get tired, even after a long day at school. Around other people, he is incredibly friendly, always smiling, joking and making everyone laugh. In fact, I think this child is special because his cheerful energy is genuinely contagious. In the end, whenever I see him, my own mood lifts immediately.

q1.html:
Q1: Why do people choose to visit galleries and museums in person instead of viewing art online?

Op Generally speaking, people visit galleries in person, because seeing a physical masterpiece (杰作) is much more impactful. Re This is due to the fact that, computer screens cannot accurately display the true aesthetic (审美的 / 美学的) texture of oil paint. Ex For example, standing in front of a real painting allows for a much deeper emotional appreciation (欣赏 / 鉴赏) of the artwork.

q2.html:
Q2: Should galleries and museums be free of charge to the public?

Op In my view, museums should absolutely be free, as art must be accessible to everyone in society. Re The primary reason is that, charging high ticket prices excludes poor families from gaining cultural wisdom (智慧). Ex As an illustration, free public galleries help to paint a picture (描绘) of history for children who cannot afford private lessons.

q3.html:
Q3: Why do some people find life inspiration from visiting these places?

Op Without a doubt, exhibitions provide immense inspiration (灵感), because they showcase the highest limits of human creativity. Re This is crucial since, viewing historical artifacts gives people a highly objective (客观的) perspective on their own modern struggles. Ex Consequently, an exhausted worker might feel highly motivated after seeing how ancient people built beautiful statues from nothing.

q4.html:
Q4: Why do most children draw more often than adults do?

Op Admittedly, children draw constantly to express themselves, as they have fewer language skills than grown adults. Re Furthermore, adults are usually more down to earth (务实的) and busy with stressful jobs to sit and paint. Ex For instance, a child will freely draw a purple dog, proving that beauty is in the eye of the beholder (情人眼里出西施) without caring about rules.

q5.html:
Q5: Is art education in schools as important as science or mathematics?

Op Undeniably, art education is equally important, because it actively develops a student's creative problem-solving skills. Re While math teaches strict logic, art teaches kids how to invent new ideas and express complex emotions properly. Ex Therefore, ignoring the arts creates robotic students who lack the innovative thinking needed for future societal progress.

q6.html:
Q6: How does engaging in artistic activities contribute to the cognitive development of a society?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, artistic activities boost cognitive (认知的) development, as they challenge the brain to think visually. Re This essentially means that, communities that support the arts tend to produce more forward-thinking engineers and designers. Ex For example, learning to play musical instruments improves memory and spatial awareness, making the whole society much smarter.

q1.html:
Describe an interesting building you'd like to visit. You should say:
Where it is
How you knew about it
What you know about it
And explain how you will feel if you go there.

To begin with, I really want to visit a magnificent (宏伟的) museum in Beijing, because I love history. Actually, this iconic (标志性的) building is a famous landmark (地标) that will definitely stand the test of time (经受住时间的考验). I heard that, the inside is extremely spacious (宽敞的) and filled with very imaginative (富于想象力的) artworks. Even though, the exterior looks slightly dilapidated (破旧的), it holds many contemporary (当代的) exhibits. If I go there, I will be very observant (观察力敏锐的) to catch every tiny detail. I think, seeing it in person will totally take one's breath away (令人叹为观止).

q2.html:
Describe a shop or market you enjoy visiting. You should say:
What the shop or market is
Where it is
What you can buy there
And explain why you enjoy going to this place.

Honestly, I want to talk about a lovely old bookshop that I really enjoy visiting. To begin with, this small shop is located just near my school, so I drop by almost every week after class. Actually, you can buy all sorts of things there, like used novels, beautiful notebooks, and small handmade gifts for friends. In fact, the prices are quite reasonable too. To be honest, what I love most is the quiet atmosphere inside, because it lets me relax and read in peace. In the end, this shop truly feels like my second home.

q3.html:
Describe an old building or place in your hometown that has special meaning. You should say:
What the building is
How old it is
What makes it special
And explain why this building is important to the local community.

To begin with, I want to talk about an ancient temple in my hometown that holds deep meaning for everyone. Actually, this temple has stood for over 200 years, so it has truly survived many generations. In fact, what makes it really special is its beautiful design, with carved wooden doors and elegant stone statues that look amazing. Honestly, local people gather here during festivals to pray and celebrate together. To be honest, this place is important because it gives the community a strong sense of cultural pride. In the end, it really connects everyone to our shared history.

q1.html:
Q1: Should all museums and historical buildings sell tickets?

Op Generally speaking, museums should sell tickets, as they need funds to maintain their beautiful heritage (遗产 / 传统). Re This is because, the daily preservation (保护 / 维护) of ancient artifacts requires a massive financial budget. Ex For instance, using ticket money helps hire experts to restore an old painting so it remains a true masterpiece (杰作).

q2.html:
Q2: Which kind of building do people prefer, villas or apartments?

Op In my view, wealthy people prefer villas, while most standard citizens live in high-rise apartments. Re The primary reason is that, a city skyscraper (摩天大楼) provides affordable housing for thousands of daily workers. Ex As an illustration, living in a crowded residential (居住的 / 住宅的) block is the only way to get a roof over one's head (栖身之处) in Beijing.

q3.html:
Q3: Why do people prefer to live in new houses?

Op Without a doubt, people prefer new houses, because they offer modern comfort and better safety features. Re This is largely due to the fact that, newly built homes have highly upgraded infrastructure (基础设施) like smart heating. Ex For example, older properties often have leaking pipes and broken windows, which are extremely expensive to fix.

q4.html:
Q4: Which do people prefer, modern buildings or traditional buildings?

Op Admittedly, young people prefer modern designs, whereas the older generation deeply values traditional architecture. Re The core issue is that, modern offices are practical, but ancient temples have a unique aesthetic (美学的) charm. Ex Consequently, modern glass towers turn the city into a cold concrete jungle (钢筋水泥森林), which many older citizens strongly dislike.

q5.html:
Q5: How does the architecture of a city influence the cultural identity of its residents?

Op Undeniably, architecture shapes identity, as local buildings constantly remind people of their shared history. Re When a city protects its ancient walls, it helps to successfully build bridges (建立关系 / 沟通) between the past and present. Ex Therefore, growing up around traditional courtyards makes citizens feel incredibly proud of their unique local culture.

q6.html:
Q6: Is it necessary to preserve old buildings even if they impede modern urban development?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, preserving old buildings is essential, because history cannot be replaced once destroyed. Re This means that, developers should carefully build around historical sites rather than tearing them down for quick money. Ex For instance, European cities successfully mix ancient churches with new train stations without losing their charm.

q1.html:
Describe a technology product you would like to own. You should say:
What it is
How much it costs
What you will use it for
And explain why you would like to own it.

To begin with, I really want to buy a cutting-edge (尖端的 / 前沿的) laptop, because my current computer is completely obsolete (过时的 / 淘汰的), and I urgently need an upgrade. Actually, this new device is a highly versatile (多功能的) tool that is absolutely perfect for school. Even though it is quite compact (紧凑的 / 小巧的), the internal hard drive is incredibly spacious (宽敞的). I know that it is extremely pricey (昂贵的) and will definitely cost an arm and a leg (极其昂贵). However, the magnificent (宏伟的) screen makes it a definite must-have (必备品) for my daily homework. Ultimately, owning this state-of-the-art (最先进的) machine would make my study sessions much more productive.

q2.html:
Describe a gift you received that uses modern technology. You should say:
What the gift was
Who gave it to you
How you use it in daily life
And explain why this gift is special to you.

Honestly, I want to talk about a wonderful smart speaker I received last year, which has completely changed my mornings. To begin with, my uncle gave it to me as a birthday present, and he picked it out himself. Actually, I use it almost every single day to play music while I am getting ready for school, and sometimes to check the weather. In fact, it can also answer simple questions and set timers when I am studying. In the end, this gift feels really special because it shows how thoughtful my uncle is and how well he knows my daily habits.

q3.html:
Describe an old item you own that still works well. You should say:
What the item is
How long you have had it
What you use it for
And explain why you have not replaced it.

To begin with, I want to talk about my old MP3 player, which is still one of my favourite gadgets at home. Actually, I have had it for about six years now, since my parents bought it for me back in middle school. Honestly, I mainly use it to listen to music when I go jogging in the park or before I fall asleep at night. In fact, it still sounds really great and the battery lasts surprisingly long. In the end, I have not replaced it because it works perfectly fine and it reminds me of my younger days.

q1.html:
Q1: What tech products are your friends using these days?

Op Generally speaking, my friends are currently obsessed with Virtual (虚拟的) reality headsets, because they completely Revolutionize (彻底变革) modern gaming. Re This is because, these immersive devices allow young players to experience entirely new worlds right from their bedrooms. Ex For example, instead of a boring Distraction (干扰 / 分心), they cleverly use it to explore ancient historical Heritage (遗产) sites online.

q2.html:
Q2: Why do people love using tech products?

Op In my view, people genuinely love modern gadgets, as they provide massive convenience and endless entertainment options. Re The primary reason is that, heavy tasks that previously took hours can now be completed At the click of a button (弹指之间 / 极其便捷). Ex As an illustration, we can instantly order hot food or seamlessly book taxis without ever leaving our comfortable homes.

q3.html:
Q3: What are the differences between chatting online and chatting face-to-face?

Op Undeniably, face-to-face communication builds deeper emotional bonds, whereas texting often causes unfortunate interpersonal misunderstandings. Re This is largely due to the fact that, digital messages completely lack essential physical body language and vocal tone. Ex Consequently, relying solely on internet chatting frequently leads to a severe sense of social Isolation (孤立) among teenagers.

q4.html:
Q4: Has technology widened the economic gap between the rich and the poor?

Op Admittedly, technology has heavily widened the wealth gap, as expensive devices are strictly limited to affluent (富裕的) families. Re However, a clear Silver lining (一线希望 / 坏事中的好事) is that cheap smartphones now give poor students excellent access to free education. Ex For instance, upgrading digital Infrastructure (基础设施) in rural villages helps impoverished (贫困的) children finally learn critical skills online.

q5.html:
Q5: Do you think people are losing their ability to communicate effectively due to screens?

Op Without a doubt, heavy screen usage severely damages our social skills, because we practice real conversations much less frequently. Re This is primarily since, younger generations constantly text rather than speaking directly just to Keep up with the times (与时俱进). Ex Therefore, many teenagers feel extremely awkward and painfully anxious when they have to order food face-to-face.

q6.html:
Q6: To what extent should governments regulate the development of Artificial Intelligence?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, governments must strictly regulate Artificial Intelligence (人工智能), as it possesses highly dangerous capabilities. Re The core issue is that, unregulated algorithms (算法) could easily manipulate (操纵) public opinions or steal private user data. Ex For example, creating strict international laws effectively prevents wealthy tech companies from secretly abusing these powerful new computer systems.

q1.html:
Describe an occasion when you lost your way. You should say:
Where you were
What happened
How you felt
And explain how you found your way.

To begin with, I clearly remember getting completely lost in Beijing, because I was wandering through an unfamiliar (不熟悉的) neighborhood. Actually, I was trying to find a famous restaurant but suddenly felt extremely disoriented (迷失方向的). Because the tiny streets looked identical, I started to go around in circles (原地打转 / 毫无进展). At first, I was terribly panicked (惊慌失措的) and thought finding my way back was a lost cause (注定失败的事 / 没救了). Luckily, I used my compact (小巧的) and versatile (多功能的) smartphone to ask a helpful (有帮助的) local police officer for directions. Ultimately, I felt so relieved (如释重负的) when I finally arrived back at my hotel safe and sound (安然无恙).

q2.html:
Describe a time when you were in an unfamiliar place and felt nervous. You should say:
Where the place was
Why you were there
What made you feel nervous
And explain how you dealt with the situation.

Honestly, I want to talk about a time I arrived alone in a brand new city for a job interview. To begin with, I had never visited this place before, so everything looked completely strange to me. Actually, I was there to take an important exam connected to the position. What made me really nervous was that I could not find my way to the test center, and the streets all looked identical. In the end, I quickly opened a phone map app and followed the directions carefully. Luckily, I arrived just in time and felt much calmer afterwards.

q3.html:
Describe a journey where everything went wrong. You should say:
Where you were going
Who you were with
What went wrong during the trip
And explain how you felt at the end of the journey.

To begin with, I want to share a memorable trip I took last spring to visit my relatives in another province. Actually, I traveled together with my whole family, including my parents and younger brother. Unfortunately, almost everything went wrong from the very beginning. Our train was seriously late, so we missed our connection and had to wait for hours at a crowded station. On top of that, our luggage was sent to the wrong platform. However, in the end, we finally arrived safely and my relatives welcomed us warmly. Honestly, despite the chaos, it had a happy ending.

q1.html:
Q1: Why do some people get lost more easily than others?

Op Generally speaking, some individuals get lost easily, because they naturally lack a basic sense of spatial Orientation (方向感). Re This is because, they completely ignore their surroundings and rely entirely on digital Distraction (分心) like texting. Ex For instance, someone who never pays attention to a physical Landmark (地标) will severely struggle to memorize any new city route.

q2.html:
Q2: Do you think it is important to do some preparation before you travel to new places?

Op In my view, thorough preparation is absolutely vital, as it ensures you remain safe in completely unknown environments. Re The primary reason is that, doing research helps you quickly Find one's feet (适应新环境 / 站稳脚跟) in a foreign culture. Ex However, some brave backpackers wildly prefer zero planning, simply deciding to Cross that bridge when you come to it (船到桥头自然直).

q3.html:
Q3: How can people find their way when they are lost?

Op Without a doubt, using mobile phone Navigation (导航) apps is easily the most effective method for lost travelers. Re This is largely due to the fact that, GPS technology can pinpoint your exact global location almost instantly. Ex Alternatively, if they have no internet connection, they must trust their own Intuition (直觉) or bravely ask a local police officer.

q4.html:
Q4: Is a paper map still necessary?

Op Admittedly, paper maps are rarely used daily, but they remain strictly necessary for severe emergency backup situations. Re The core issue is that, smart phones can easily run out of battery or lose signal in rural mountains. Ex Consequently, if you hike entirely Off the beaten track (人迹罕至的), a physical map is the only reliable survival tool.

q5.html:
Q5: Has the reliance on GPS technology reduced our natural ability to navigate?

Op Undeniably, our heavy Reliance (依赖) on GPS has severely diminished our brain's natural mapping abilities over time. Re As people blindly follow screen directions, they completely stop memorizing street names or observing the actual physical world. Ex Therefore, if the Virtual (虚拟的) map crashes, most young drivers are completely incapable of driving themselves back home safely.

q6.html:
Q6: How does getting "lost" (metaphorically) in a new culture help a person grow?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, struggling in a new culture intensely builds personal resilience (韧性) and extreme patience. Re This is necessary since, navigating entirely unfamiliar social rules forces individuals to step far outside their comfortable boundaries. Ex For example, accidentally ordering the wrong food teaches travelers to embrace exciting mistakes and ultimately become much more adaptable.

q1.html:
Describe a famous person you would like to meet. You should say:
Who he/she is
How you know about him/her
How/where you would like to meet him/her
And explain why you would like to meet him/her.

To begin with, I would absolutely love to meet a highly influential (有影响力的) athlete named Yao Ming, because he is a true basketball legend. Actually, I first learned about this amazingly talented (有才华的) player from watching his legendary (传奇的) NBA games on television. If I met him, I would ask for some helpful (有帮助的) advice on how to improve my own sports skills. I think, despite always being in the limelight (处于聚光灯下 / 受人关注), he remains incredibly humble (谦逊的) and firmly down to earth (接地气的 / 务实的). Furthermore, his charismatic (有魅力的) personality makes him a perfect role model (榜样) for teenagers. Ultimately, I would feel so relieved (如释重负的) and happy to shake his giant hand.

q2.html:
Describe a person you know who has a special talent. You should say:
Who this person is
What their special talent is
How they developed this talent
And explain why you think this talent is impressive.

Honestly, I want to talk about my classmate Lily, who is an absolutely amazing painter. To begin with, she sits next to me in art class, and we have been good friends for many years. Actually, her special talent is creating beautiful watercolor paintings of nature scenes that look incredibly realistic. She developed this skill by practicing every single day after school, sometimes for three or four hours straight. In fact, she also watched many online tutorials to improve her techniques. In the end, I think her talent is so impressive because she turned simple hard work into real artistic magic.

q3.html:
Describe a historical figure you admire and would like to learn more about. You should say:
Who this person is
What they are famous for
How you first learned about them
And explain why you admire this person.

To begin with, I would love to talk about Qian Xuesen, a brilliant Chinese scientist who lived in the twentieth century. Actually, he is famous for being the founding father of China's space and missile programs, which completely changed our country's future. I first learned about him during a history class in middle school, when our teacher showed us a touching documentary about his life. Honestly, I was deeply moved by his story. In the end, I admire him because he gave up a comfortable life abroad and bravely served his country, contributing his incredible knowledge to help China grow stronger.

q1.html:
Q1: What are the advantages and disadvantages of being a famous child?

Op Generally speaking, famous children gain massive wealth early, but sadly lose their normal childhood Privacy (隐私) completely. Re This is because, their entire life is documented by ruthless Paparazzi (狗仔队) everywhere they go daily. Ex For instance, child actors often Live in a fishbowl (毫无隐私的生活), which inevitably causes severe anxiety and strongly prevents them from making genuine friends.

Q3: Do young people appreciate antiques and old things as much as older people?

Op Undeniably, the modern younger generation strongly prefers shiny new gadgets, whereas wise seniors truly deeply respect historical cultural artifacts. Re This is largely due to the fact that, busy young minds vastly prioritize immediate digital convenience entirely over long-term physical historical preservation. Ex Consequently, only a highly rare few educated teenagers successfully view a vintage mechanical watch as a genuinely wise financial Investment (投资).

q4.html:
Q4: Why do people like to go to museums to see old objects?

Op Admittedly, curious citizens eagerly visit grand museums to rapidly escape daily stress, because quietly viewing ancient tools satisfies deep human curiosity. Re The core issue is that, closely observing exactly how our strong ancestors creatively survived physically grounds us heavily in a chaotic modern world. Ex Therefore, people happily eagerly pay the easy Affordability (购买力) of a remarkably cheap ticket to properly educate their young children on local history.

q5.html:
Q5: Is the culture of consumerism (buying things for pleasure) sustainable for the planet?

Op Without a doubt, extreme Consumerism (消费主义) is utterly catastrophic (灾难性的), as it rapidly fatally depletes Earth's highly finite global natural resources. Re This is primarily since, the deeply blind pursuit of endless cheap luxury goods completely destroys all realistic hopes for global environmental Sustainability (可持续性). Ex For example, aggressively producing billions of cheap plastic toys unfortunately turns the entire developing world into a highly toxic Throwaway society (用完即弃的社会).

q6.html:
Q6: Does the "planned obsolescence" of products (products designed to break) harm society?

Op It is widely acknowledged that, intentionally secretly breaking products heavily harms society, because it economically ruthlessly punishes poor struggling families. Re This essentially means that, deeply greedy tech companies actively force loyal consumers to act exactly like a desperate trapped Hoarder (囤积狂) of useless broken chargers. Ex For instance, purposely designing smartphones that deliberately legally slow down after two years causes massive financial stress and terrible electronic toxic waste.

q1.html:
Describe a long journey you want to enjoy again. You should say:
When you took the journey
Where you went
Who you were with
And explain why you want to go again.

To begin with, I want to describe a scenic (风景优美的) train trip I took to Yunnan, which was completely spontaneous (自发的 / 随性的), because I needed a break. Honestly, it was quite a bumpy ride (颠簸的旅程 / 坎坷) since the train was delayed (延误的) by rain. However, I decided to travel light (轻装上阵) so carrying my bags wasn't exhausting (筋疲力尽的). Furthermore, I felt very adventurous (爱冒险的) exploring the local villages. In retrospect, it was a deeply sentimental (情感的) experience for me. Ultimately, I gained some valuable (宝贵的) memories, and I would love to hit the road (上路 / 出发) and do it again.

q2.html:
Describe a bicycle, motorcycle, or car trip you would like to go on. You should say:
Who you would like to go with
Where you would like to go
Which vehicle you would choose
And explain why you would like to travel this way.

Honestly, I would love to go on a mountain biking trip with my best friend from university. To begin with, we have always shared a passion for outdoor adventures, so he would be the perfect companion. Actually, I would choose a forest trail somewhere in the mountains of Zhejiang, because the scenery there is absolutely stunning. For the vehicle, I would definitely pick a sturdy mountain bike, since it can handle rough terrain easily. In the end, I want to travel this way because cycling helps me stay healthy, reduces my carbon footprint, and gives me a real sense of freedom.

q3.html:
Describe a time your travel plans were delayed or changed. You should say:
Where you were going
What caused the delay
What you did while waiting
And explain how you felt about the change in plans.

To begin with, I want to talk about a trip I took last winter when I was heading to Shanghai for a family reunion. Actually, I was at the train station early in the morning, feeling excited about the journey ahead. However, due to heavy snowfall, I missed my connection and had to wait nearly four hours for the next available train. While waiting, I ate lunch at a small noodle shop and read a book on my phone. Honestly, I felt quite annoyed and frustrated at first, but in the end, I learned to be more patient with travel.

q1.html:
Q1: Who prefers long journeys, young people or old people?

Op Generally speaking, young people prefer long journeys, whereas older individuals usually avoid them. Re To explain, the youth have more energy for eco-tourism (生态旅游) and care less about their carbon footprint (碳足迹) when traveling far. Ex For instance, many university students take month-long backpacking trips across different continents.

q2.html:
Q2: What do people prepare before a journey?

Op Undoubtedly, travelers pack essential items, and they also plan their itineraries carefully. Re This is because, thorough preparation (准备) helps them avoid the stressful hustle and bustle (熙熙攘攘) of navigating unfamiliar and crowded places. Ex For example, booking hotels and researching local transit routes in advance ensures a much smoother trip.

q3.html:
Q3: Why do people prefer traveling in their own countries rather than foreign countries?

Op In my view, domestic travel is much cheaper, and it avoids language barriers. Re Specifically, people do not want a long commute (通勤) or to deal with massive airport congestion (拥堵) during short holidays. Ex As an illustration, driving to a nearby province is significantly easier than flying internationally.

q4.html:
Q4: Do you think air pollution comes mostly from mobile vehicles?

Op To a certain extent, cars are a major source, but factories contribute heavily too. Re Evidently, industrial exhaust and vehicular emission (排放) both accumulate (积累) in the atmosphere to cause severe smog. Ex For instance, many manufacturing hubs have noticeably worse air quality than busy urban centers.

q5.html:
Q5: Do you think people need to change the way of transportation drastically to protect the environment?

Op Absolutely, a significant shift is required if we want to save our planet. Re Fundamentally, our current reliance on fossil fuels completely undermines long-term environmental sustainability (可持续性). Ex Therefore, switching globally to electric trains or hydrogen-powered buses will dramatically lower harmful pollution levels.

q6.html:
Q6: Has the ability to travel cheaply (budget airlines) changed how people view the world?

Op Certainly, budget travel has broadened perspectives, since more people can explore internationally. Re Consequently, average citizens can escape the daily rush hour (高峰期) to experience foreign cultures firsthand. Ex For example, affordable flights allow students to witness global diversity, making them much more tolerant.

q1.html:
Describe a creative person whose work you admire (e.g., artist or musician). You should say:
Who this person is
How you knew him/her
What creative work he/she does
And explain why you admire him/her.

To begin with, I want to talk about an innovative (创新的) musician I admire, who is extremely dedicated (专注的 / 献身的), and whose music is incredible. Honestly, he is completely versatile (多才多艺的), playing multiple instruments flawlessly. Furthermore, he wasn't born with a silver spoon (含着金汤匙出生), but worked hard for his success. Sometimes, his performances are highly spontaneous (自发的). Moreover, he can think on his feet (反应敏捷) when a guitar string breaks. Truly, he is an exceptional (杰出的 / 异常的) talent and a true visionary (有远见的). Additionally, his adventurous (敢于创新的) spirit makes him a natural (天生好手) on stage.

q2.html:
Describe a person you know who is good at more than one thing. You should say:
Who this person is
What things they are good at
How they learned these skills
And explain why you think they are so talented.

Honestly, I want to talk about my older cousin, who is amazingly multi-talented. To begin with, she is currently a university student, but she does so much outside of class. Actually, she is fantastic at both art and music, since she paints beautiful watercolors and also plays the violin really well. Surprisingly, she is mostly self-taught, learning from free videos online and practicing every single day after school. In my opinion, she is so talented because she works very hard and never gives up easily. To be honest, watching her switch between hobbies always inspires me to try new things myself.

q3.html:
Describe someone who has an unusual or interesting hobby. You should say:
Who this person is
What their hobby is
How they started this hobby
And explain what makes this hobby special.

Actually, I'd like to talk about my neighbor, an older gentleman who lives just next door. To begin with, he has a really unusual hobby, which is building detailed model planes from tiny pieces of wood and plastic. Apparently, he started this hobby when he was a teenager, after his father gave him a small kit as a birthday present. Honestly, what makes it special is how creative and patient he is, since each plane takes him weeks to finish. In the end, his living room looks like a small museum, and visitors are always impressed by his amazing work.

q1.html:
Q1: How can we know if a child has talent or not?

Op Essentially, parents can spot talent early, especially when children show innate (天生的) skills. Re Specifically, a child might naturally excel at tasks without needing intense preparation (准备). Ex For example, a brainy (聪明的) toddler who solves complex puzzles easily demonstrates high intellectual potential (潜力).

q2.html:
Q2: Is it a waste of time if people don't use their talents?

Op Honestly, it is a tragic loss, because society misses out on their gifts. Re Therefore, if we do not nurture (培养 / 后天教育) our abilities, we fail to contribute meaningfully. Ex For instance, a musical genius who ignores their gift limits their own creative emission (排放) into the world.

q3.html:
Q3: Are children born smart, or do they become smart through learning?

Op In reality, it is a combination, since both genetics and environment matter. Re Certainly, natural intelligence (智力) exists, but education refines it because practice makes perfect (熟能生巧). Ex As an example, rigorous academic (学术的) training can make an average student highly successful over time.

q4.html:
Q4: Is being intelligent always a good thing? Are there limitations?

Op Admittedly, high intellect is beneficial, yet it can cause social isolation. Re Frequently, brilliant individuals focus on one niche instead of being a jack of all trades (万金油 / 博而不精), causing them to struggle socially. Ex For example, a math prodigy (神童) might feel completely misunderstood by regular classmates.

q5.html:
Q5: Do you think the education system stifles creativity by focusing too much on academic scores?

Op Absolutely, strict testing restricts imagination, and it limits independent thought. Re Consequently, schools reward memorization (死记硬背) rather than original problem-solving skills. Ex For instance, art and music programs are often canceled to make more time for standardized test preparation.

q6.html:
Q6: Can creativity be learned, or is it an innate quality?

Op Personally, I believe it can be developed, although some have natural inclinations (倾向). Re Fundamentally, being exposed to diverse experiences teaches the brain to form new connections. Ex For example, taking painting classes can unlock a person's hidden artistic abilities over time.

q1.html:
Describe a person you know who works in a family business. You should say:
Who he/she is
What the business is (e.g., store/restaurant)
What products it sells
And explain what you have learned from observing this business.

First of all, I want to describe a diligent (勤奋的) baker I know, who is highly entrepreneurial (有创业精神的), and who owns a great shop. Essentially, his bakery is a traditional mom and pop store (夫妻店). Currently, his aging father continues to run the show (掌管 / 主持大局). However, they plan to pass the torch (薪火相传 / 交接) to him soon. Honestly, they sell authentic (正宗的 / 真实的) sourdough bread that is universally loved. Furthermore, their business is extremely prosperous (繁荣的) due to their hard work. Overall, they are a very reputable (声誉好的) and dedicated (敬业的) family, providing exceptional (杰出的) service.

q2.html:
Describe a restaurant or cafe that you enjoy going to. You should say:
Where it is
What food or drinks it serves
How often you go there
And explain why you like this place.

Honestly, I want to talk about a small noodle restaurant that I really enjoy going to. To begin with, it is located just near my school, which makes it super convenient to drop by after class. Actually, they serve all kinds of traditional noodles, along with side dishes and homemade soup. I usually go there about twice a week with my classmates, especially on busy days. The reason I like this place so much is that the food is incredibly tasty and the prices are reasonable. In the end, it really feels like a second home to me.

q3.html:
Describe a place in your city that has changed a lot over the years. You should say:
What the place is
What it used to be like
How it has changed
And explain whether you think the change is good or bad.

Honestly, I want to describe an old market street in the centre of my city that has transformed dramatically. To begin with, this place used to be full of small old shops selling vegetables, snacks, and handmade goods. Actually, it had a really lively, traditional atmosphere where everyone knew each other. However, in recent years, it has become quite modern, with chain stores, cafes, and shopping malls replacing the original vendors. To be honest, I have mixed feelings about this change. On the one hand, it looks cleaner and more convenient, but on the other hand, the old charm is gone forever.

q1.html:
Q1: Would you like to start a family business?

Op Personally, I would avoid it, because mixing family and work is stressful. Re Specifically, managing a startup (初创公司) requires intense focus that can easily damage personal relationships. Ex For example, arguments over financial decisions might cause a family to go out of business (破产 / 倒闭).

q2.html:
Q2: What are the advantages and disadvantages of family businesses?

Op Generally, they offer deep trust, yet they often lack professional management. Re Undoubtedly, competition (竞争) is fierce, and relatives may not have the innate (固有的) skills to survive. Ex For instance, a small family shop might lose out to a larger, more efficient corporation (大公司 / 企业).

q3.html:
Q3: Why do young people start their own business?

Op Mainly, they desire independence, and they want to pursue their passions. Re Furthermore, the modern economy (经济) offers immense potential (潜力) for innovative ideas to succeed quickly. Ex As an illustration, many tech graduates create software apps to become their own bosses.

q4.html:
Q4: How do large international corporations impact local family-owned businesses?

Op Sadly, they often destroy local shops, as they offer much lower prices. Re Essentially, it is a cut-throat (残酷的) environment where big fish eat little fish (大鱼吃小鱼 / 弱肉强食). Ex For example, a giant supermarket opening nearby usually bankrupts small neighborhood grocery stores.

q5.html:
Q5: Is it better for a city's economy to have many small shops or a few large shopping malls?

Op In my opinion, diverse small shops are better, because they distribute wealth evenly. Re Conversely, a monopoly (垄断) by massive malls removes character from the city streets. Ex Therefore, supporting local vendors keeps the community vibrant and financially stable.

q6.html:
Q6: How has the rise of online shopping changed the physical landscape of city centers?

Op Noticeably, many physical stores have closed down, and streets look emptier. Re As a result, high retail rents force traditional brands to move entirely to e-commerce platforms. Ex For instance, former shopping districts are now being converted into residential apartments or cafes.

q1.html:
Describe a toy that you loved in your childhood. You should say:
What the toy was
Who gave it to you
How often you played with it
And explain why it was so special to you.

To begin with, I will describe my wooden train set, which was highly educational (有教育意义的), and which kept me busy. Honestly, looking at it now takes me on a trip down memory lane (追忆往昔).
